user_query:
I'm gearing up for a flashy pre-show release of my "HypeClip Showdown" app and need a tight, verifiable DevOps check-up I can brag about on stream. Please use the provided local files to analyze our CI/CD pipeline and produce two artifacts:

Inputs (all required):
- input/ci/runs.jsonl — job-level CI run records. Each line is a JSON object with: timestamp, workflow_name, run_id, job_name, status, conclusion, duration_seconds, attempt, event.
- input/.github/workflows/build.yml — our CI workflow config.
- input/Dockerfile — the app image build definition.
- input/env/production.yaml — production app config and feature flags.

Outputs (create these):
1) output/ci_metrics.csv — One row per workflow_name with columns, in this exact order:
   - workflow_name
   - total_runs
   - successful_runs
   - success_rate (successful_runs / total_runs, rounded to two decimals)
   - avg_run_duration_seconds (average duration per run, rounded to nearest integer)
   - rerun_rate (fraction of runs where any job had attempt > 1, rounded to two decimals)

   Definitions for metrics (apply exactly):
   - Treat records sharing the same workflow_name and run_id as one workflow run.
   - For each job_name within a run_id, select only the record with the highest attempt to represent the job’s final outcome and duration.
   - A run is successful if all selected job records have conclusion == "success".
   - Run duration = sum of duration_seconds of the selected (highest-attempt) job records within that run.

2) output/release_status.md — A concise pre-show release status note with the following sections and content:
   - Title: "Pre-Show Release CI Status — HypeClip Showdown"
   - Pipeline Summary: a brief table or bullet list, one line per workflow_name, showing total_runs, success_rate, avg_run_duration_seconds, and rerun_rate from output/ci_metrics.csv.
   - Slowest Jobs: the top 3 job_names ranked by average duration across runs (use only the highest-attempt record per job per run for this calculation). Show job_name and average_duration_seconds (rounded to nearest integer).
   - Config Observations: 
     • Compare the Node.js version(s) used in input/.github/workflows/build.yml (actions/setup-node steps) to the base Node version in input/Dockerfile. If the major versions differ, clearly flag a mismatch and state both versions.
     • State whether dependency caching is configured in the CI (e.g., actions/cache). If absent, note that caching is not configured.
   - App Config Snapshot: list application_version and the feature_flags from input/env/production.yaml.
   - Action Items (at least these two bullets):
     • Align Node.js version between CI and Dockerfile.
     • Add dependency caching to CI to speed up installs.

Notes:
- All calculations must be derived from the provided files; do not assume anything not present in inputs.
- The CSV must have the exact column order specified.
- The report should be readable and punchy, but the required sections and facts must be present.
- Save outputs exactly at output/ci_metrics.csv and output/release_status.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
